a.label: Explicit surface (Monge patch)
a.standard_term: Monge patch / graph hypersurface x_n = f(x_1,...,x_{n-1})

b.label: Self/coupling Hessian split H = H_c + H_s
b.standard_term: diagonal-plus-hollow (diagonal / zero-diagonal) matrix decomposition of the Hessian

evidence.label: Hessian structure argument (proof)
evidence.summary: The Hessian of F = x_n − f(x₁, ..., x_{n-1}) has a zero n-th row and n-th column (except for the diagonal entry H_nn which is also zero for explicit surfaces). This structure causes all H_c · H_s cross-products to vanish

evidence.label: Hessian structure for explicit surfaces F = z − f(x,y)
evidence.summary: For any constraint of the form F(x,y,z) = z − f(x,y) = 0, the interaction curvature vanishes exactly. This is because the Hessian has the structure: H = | −f_xx  −f_xy   0 | | −f_xy  −f_yy   0 | |   0      0     0 | The
